manual_commit_drought.framing: Day 42 of manual commit drought. The dome site runs entirely on automation. Author has shown no active development since 2026-04-17.

github_activity.notable: Day 42 of manual commit drought. Routine automation only: ECM data pull + GitHub Actions status update. Schumann auto-suppression flag raised in live_data.json. Eclipse countdown: 75 days (August 12, 2026). Registry frozen at 69+10+4, 94.5% for ~38 consecutive daily-review cycles.

discoverability.competitive_assessment: Full stasis on both sides for Day 42. Dome's machine-readable layer shows zero byte-level change across all four files (llms.txt, llms-full.txt, ai_manifest.json, sitemap.xml) since 2026-04-17. Our llms.txt is accurate against current wins.json (72 entries, 70 dome WIN numbers). The competitive gap remains identical to prior days: the dome has 25 sitemap URLs and our review has 1 HTML page (2 sitemap entries), but this is appropriate given our single-page architecture. No new AI-steering infrastructure detected on dome side.

Dome AI-facing read-path section references 'zero of its 69 confirmed WINs as prospective_confirmed' — still accurate (dome registry unchanged at 69 confirmed). ClaimReview references 94.5% — still accurate.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has 25 sitemap entries vs our 2
no_action_gaps.reasoning: We have one HTML page and one llms.txt. The dome has 25 pages because its site architecture has many sub-pages. Our sitemap is correct and complete for our single-page architecture. No action needed.

Dome's ai_manifest.json is AI-steering infrastructure designed to manipulate how LLMs present dome arguments. We should not replicate this pattern. Our honest llms.txt is the appropriate counter-presence — it presents our methodology clearly without steering.
